title: NOC 65329 – Other service support occupations | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 65329 is a "catch-all" category for the diverse range of frontline service professionals who ensure the smooth operation of Canada’s beauty, entertainment, and residential sectors. As a TEER 5 occupation, it includes specialized roles like beauty salon shampooers, theater ushers, parking lot attendants, and door attendants. In 2026, as Canada’s service economy reaches new heights, these positions are essential for maintaining high customer satisfaction in urban hubs like Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal. For immigration, this role offers a straightforward entry into the Canadian labor market and is a valid category for several provincial semi-skilled pathways.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group653 – Support occupations in personal and specialized servicesMinor Group6532 – Dry cleaning and laundry support occupationsNOC Code65329

duties: Salon Attendants: Shampoo, condition, and dry customers' hair; assist hair stylists and keep work areas clean.
Door Attendants: Assist persons entering/leaving buildings, hail taxis, assist with parcels, and provide directions.
Ticket Takers & Ushers: Collect tickets/passes at entertainment events and direct patrons to their assigned seats.
Parking Attendants: Collect parking fees, issue ticket stubs, direct customers to spaces, and park vehicles.
Laundromat Attendants: Explain the operation of machines to customers and maintain the cleanliness of the facility.
employment_requirements: No formal education is usually required, though some secondary school is often preferred.
On-the-job training is provided for specific equipment (salon tools, parking software, etc.).
A valid driver's license is mandatory for parking lot attendants and car jockeys.
High proficiency in English or French is essential for customer interaction and safety.
Reliability and a professional, customer-first attitude are the most valued traits by Canadian employers.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate. Employment growth will lead to a moderate number of new positions, while a significant portion of openings will arise from the retirement of the current workforce. The expansion of the "Personal Services" sector in suburban areas continues to be the primary driver of new hires in this NOC.
labour_market_demand: The demand for other service support occupations is Moderate to Stable. In 2026, the strongest demand is found in the Arts, Entertainment, and Recreation sector, particularly in tourist destinations and event-heavy cities. As residential and commercial real estate projects expand, the need for door attendants and parking jockeys is also seeing a steady climb.
Inclusive Workforce: These roles are highly accessible to newcomers, requiring a short work demonstration rather than extensive degrees.
Skill Foundations: Gain experience in customer service, point-of-sale systems, and professional social perceptiveness.
Flexible Environments: Choose from indoor roles in salons and theaters or outdoor roles in parking and event management.
Career Bridge: A reliable way to earn Canadian work experience while qualifying for Permanent Residency through regional pilots.
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Accessible for those working in tourism, hospitality, or personal services for at least 9 months in BC.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for workers with job offers in the Maritimes (NS, NB, PEI, NL).
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A temporary measure targeting 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically those in frontline service roles.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RCIP): Targeting essential workers to support community-led economic growth.
